steps.title: Identify the em dash signal
steps.content: The em dash before cannot explain signals that a descriptive clause should be enclosed by matching em dashes on both sides.

steps.title: Apply the em dash enclosure rule
steps.content: A relative clause providing descriptive information must be enclosed by matching em dashes when em dashes are used. Since an em dash already precedes cannot explain, an opening em dash must precede which. Choice A provides this: models — which. Choices B, C, and D either mix inconsistent punctuation (comma in B), omit the opening mark entirely (C), or place the dash incorrectly and break the clause (D). That matches choice A — A is correct.
